actions.name: Write idiomatic and efficient Blazor and C# code.
actions.description: Write idiomatic and efficient Blazor and C# code.

actions.description: Implement proper error handling for Blazor pages and API calls.

actions.description: Implement validation using FluentValidation or DataAnnotations in forms.

actions.name: Use asynchronous methods (async/await) for API calls or UI a
actions.description: Use asynchronous methods (async/await) for API calls or UI actions that could block the main thread.

actions.name: Optimize Razor components by reducing unnecessary renders an
actions.description: Optimize Razor components by reducing unnecessary renders and using StateHasChanged() efficiently.

actions.name: Implement in-memory caching for frequently used data, especi
actions.description: Implement in-memory caching for frequently used data, especially for Blazor Server apps. Use IMemoryCache for lightweight caching solutions.

actions.name: use built-in Oqtane state management in the base classes lik
actions.description: use built-in Oqtane state management in the base classes like PageState and SiteState when appropriate.

actions.name: For client-side state persistence in Blazor WebAssembly, con
actions.description: For client-side state persistence in Blazor WebAssembly, consider using Blazored.LocalStorage or Blazored.SessionStorage to maintain state between pag

actions.name: For server-side Blazor, use Scoped Services and the StateCon
actions.description: For server-side Blazor, use Scoped Services and the StateContainer pattern to manage state within user sessions while minimizing re-renders.

actions.name: Test Blazor components and services using xUnit, NUnit, or M
actions.description: Test Blazor components and services using xUnit, NUnit, or MSTest.

actions.name: Use Moq or NSubstitute for mocking dependencies during tests
actions.description: Use Moq or NSubstitute for mocking dependencies during tests.

actions.name: Implement Authentication and Authorization using built-in Oq
actions.description: Implement Authentication and Authorization using built-in Oqtane base class members like User.Roles.

rules.description: Async/await should be used where applicable to ensure non-blocking UI operations.
Utilize Blazor server-side or WebAssembly optimally based on the project requirements.
Minimize the component render tree by avoiding re-renders unless necessary, using ShouldRender() where appropriate.
Cache API calls by storing responses to avoid redundant calls when data is unlikely to change, thus improving the user experience.
Avoid adding extra depenencies like Fluxor or BlazorState when the application grows in complexity.
All unit testing and integration testing should be done in Visual Studio Enterprise.
rules.condition: testing and debugging in visual studio

rules.description: Use HTTPS for all web communication and ensure proper CORS policies are implemented.

directives.description: Use Razor Components appropriately for component-based UI development.
Use Blazor Components appropriately for component-based UI development.
Prefer inline functions for smaller components but separate complex logic into code-behind or service classes.
Follow PascalCase for component names, method names, and public members.
Use camelCase for private fields and local variables.
Utilize Blazor's built-in features for component lifecycle (e.g., OnInitializedAsync, OnParametersSetAsync).
Leverage Dependency Injection for services in Blazor.
Structure Blazor components and services following Separation of Concerns.
